title: Renaissance art was a weapon - Ada Palmer
summary: Ada Palmer presents Renaissance art as a deliberate weapon of cultural and political influence rather than purely aesthetic expression. The discussion explores how art served strategic purposes in shaping ideas and power structures during the Renaissance period.
writeup: Ada Palmer argues that Renaissance art functioned as a deliberate instrument of cultural and political influence, not as purely aesthetic expression. The conversation traces how artworks served strategic ends, shaping ideas and reinforcing power structures during the Renaissance. The takeaway: aesthetic production during this period operated as a tool for projecting and contesting power.
